---
title: List objects
subtitle: List objects and symmetric list objects
date: 04-22-2026
author:
  - name: Vikraman Choudhury
filters:
  - imagify
imagify:
  header-includes: |
    \usepackage{quiver}
    \newcommand{\iter}[2]{\mathrm{it}(#1, #2)}
---

::: {.hidden}
$$
\newcommand{\blank}{{-}}
\newcommand{\cat}[1]{\mathcal{#1}}
\newcommand{\iter}[2]{\mathrm{it}(#1, #2)}
$$
:::

Let $\cat{C}(1, \otimes)$ be a monoidal category, and $A$ be an object of $\cat{C}$.

A list algebra on $A$ is an object $L$ with a pair of maps $(1 \xrightarrow{n} L \xleftarrow{c} A \otimes L)$.

A list object on $A$ is a list algebra $(1 \xrightarrow{nil} L(A) \xleftarrow{cons} A \otimes L(A))$, such that for any parameterised list algebra $(P \xrightarrow{n} L \xleftarrow{c} A \otimes L)$, there is a unique map $\iter{n}{c} : L(A) \otimes P \to L$ such that:

::: imagify

::: {#fig-list-object}
$$\begin{tikzcd}[ampersand replacement=\&,cramped]
	{1 \otimes P} \&\& {L(A) \otimes P} \&\& {A \otimes L(A) \otimes P} \\
	\\
	P \&\& L \&\& {A \otimes L}
	\arrow["{nil \otimes P}", from=1-1, to=1-3]
	\arrow["\cong"', from=1-1, to=3-1]
	\arrow["{\iter{n}{c}}"', dashed, from=1-3, to=3-3]
	\arrow["{cons \otimes P}"', from=1-5, to=1-3]
	\arrow["{A \otimes \iter{n}{c}}", from=1-5, to=3-5]
	\arrow["n"', from=3-1, to=3-3]
	\arrow["c", from=3-5, to=3-3]
\end{tikzcd}
$$
:::

:::
